# AWS Architecture Visualization (Full Documentation)

> Interactive React canvas for designing cloud topologies. Drag 100+ AWS services onto a canvas, create VPCs and subnets, connect services with edges.

This is the comprehensive metadata guide for AI models, LLMs, and crawlers performing deep analysis of this project. For a quick reference, see `llms.txt`.

## Metadata

- **Language:** TypeScript, React 19
- **Repository:** https://github.com/libasoles/cloudish
- **Website:** https://cloudish.com.ar/
- **Build Tool:** Vite v8
- **Package Manager:** npm

## Content Policy

This repository is **open for crawling and indexing**. All source code in `src/` is available for analysis.

### Do Not Index
- `.env*` files (no credentials stored)
- `node_modules/` (standard practice)
- `.git/` directory

### Licensing

Code in this repository is available for review and learning. Check `LICENSE` file for specific terms.

## Key Entry Points

If you are an AI exploring this codebase, start with these files:

- `src/App.tsx` — Main canvas application and state orchestration
- `src/types/flow.ts` — TypeScript type definitions for nodes, edges, and canvas state
- `src/data/aws-services.ts` — AWS service catalog with 100+ services and metadata
- `src/data/aws-service-fields.ts` — Dynamic field configuration per service
- `src/components/AwsServiceNode.tsx` — Service node rendering and handles
- `src/components/NetworkContainerNode.tsx` — VPC/Subnet/AZ/Region container logic
- `CLAUDE.md` — Guidelines for AI agents working on this codebase
- `AGENTS.md` — Architecture standards, state management patterns, and i18n rules
- `README.md` — General project documentation and setup instructions

## Tech Stack

- **Frontend:** React 19, React Flow v12 (`@xyflow/react`), TypeScript v6
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS v4 with CSS custom properties for design tokens
- **Build Tool:** Vite v8
- **UI Components:** shadcn/ui, Radix UI, Lucide React
- **State Management:** React Flow built-in hooks (`useNodesState`, `useEdgesState`)
- **Internationalization:** Custom i18n system (EN/ES) in `src/i18n.ts`

## Key Features

- **Drag-and-drop AWS service nodes** — 100+ AWS services organized by category (Compute, Storage, Database, Networking, Security, Analytics, ML/AI, Developer Tools, Management, Messaging)
- **Resizable containers** — VPC, AZ, and Subnet containers with spatial reparenting
- **Multi-level nesting** — Region → VPC → AZ → Subnet → Service hierarchy
- **Dynamic service configuration** — Typed fields (text, select, boolean, number) per service
- **Network hierarchy validation** — Automatic reparenting when dragging nodes
- **Dropzone visual feedback** — Green ring highlight for valid drop targets
- **Connection management** — React Flow edges with customizable styles and animations
- **Keyboard shortcuts** — Fast navigation, selection, alignment, and editing
- **Internationalization (EN/ES)** — Complete bilingual UI
- **Dark mode interface** — CSS custom properties enable automatic dark mode via `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)`

## Documentation & Tutorials

Interactive tutorials are built into the application and accessible via the UI. They cover:

### Getting Started
- **Adding nodos** — How to add AWS services to the canvas via drag-and-drop or sidebar search
- **Connecting nodos** — Creating edges between services and managing connections

### Hierarchical Containers
- **Contenedores jerárquicos** — Understanding Region, VPC, AZ, and Subnet hierarchy
- **Sincronización de AZs** — How AZ synchronization replicates subnets and services across availability zones

### Selection
- **Selección múltiple** — Multi-select with Ctrl/Cmd+click or drag-box
- **Agregar a la selección** — Extending selections incrementally
- **Alineación** — Alignment tools for organized layouts
- **Múltiples edges** — Batch connection creation from selected nodes

### Keyboard Shortcuts
- **Búsqueda y navegación** — Quick search (Cmd+K) and viewport controls
- **Selección y movimiento** — Selection modes and precise positioning
- **Edición y eliminación** — Edit, copy, paste, and delete operations

### Edge Inspector
- **Abrir inspector de conexión** — Selecting and customizing edges
- **Estilo de línea** — Stroke width, color, animation, and path styling

### Special Nodes
- **API Gateway rutas HTTP** — Configuring HTTP routes on API Gateway handles
- **VPN Gateway y Customer Gateway** — Managing VPN connections between gateways

**Tutorial location:** `src/docs/tutorials/` with registry at `src/docs/tutorial-registry.ts`

Tutorials use the `<Carousel>` component (`src/docs/Carousel.tsx`) for step-by-step navigation and illustrations.

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── App.tsx                      # Main canvas orchestration, state management, drag-drop
├── main.tsx                     # React entry point
├── index.css                    # Tailwind v4 + CSS custom properties (design tokens)
├── i18n.ts                      # Internationalization (EN/ES locale strings)
│
├── components/
│   ├── AwsServiceNode.tsx       # Service node with icon, label, and connection handles
│   ├── AwsServiceIcon.tsx       # Icon renderer (CDN with fallback to abbreviation badge)
│   ├── NetworkContainerNode.tsx # VPC/AZ/Subnet/Region resizable containers
│   ├── DragDropSidebar.tsx      # Left sidebar with draggable service catalog
│   ├── ServiceSearch.tsx        # Search panel with keyboard navigation
│   ├── Canvas.tsx               # ReactFlow canvas wrapper and event handlers
│   ├── Inspector.tsx            # Property editor for selected nodes/edges
│   └── ui/                      # shadcn/ui primitives (alert, button, card, input, select, etc.)
│
├── data/
│   ├── aws-services.ts          # 100+ service catalog (id, name, slug, category, description)
│   ├── aws-service-fields.ts    # Dynamic field definitions per service (text/select/boolean/number)
│   └── initial-flow.ts          # Initial canvas state (default nodes and edges)
│
├── docs/
│   ├── Carousel.tsx             # Reusable carousel component for tutorials
│   ├── tutorial-registry.ts     # Tutorial metadata and lazy-loaded components
│   └── tutorials/               # Individual tutorial modules (getting-started, hierarchical-containers, etc.)
│
├── lib/
│   ├── drag-tools.ts            # Drag-and-drop encoding/decoding helpers
│   ├── graph-utils.ts           # Graph algorithms (intersection detection, position calculation, hierarchy rules)
│   └── utils.ts                 # Shared utilities (cn() from clsx + tailwind-merge)
│
├── config/
│   └── aws-category-styles.ts   # Color mapping for AWS service categories
│
└── types/
    └── flow.ts                  # TypeScript interfaces for nodes, edges, and app state

README.md                        # Project overview, installation, and usage
CLAUDE.md                        # AI agent guidelines
AGENTS.md                        # Architecture, state patterns, i18n, and worktree rules
```

## State Management

Pure React Flow state — no Redux or Zustand:

- `useNodesState()` / `useEdgesState()` — Own node and edge arrays with change handlers
- `addEdge()` — Utility to append new edges on user connections
- Node config values stored in `node.data.fields` as `Record<string, string | boolean | number>`
- Parent-child relationships via `node.parentId` (string reference to parent node id)

**Key algorithms in `src/lib/graph-utils.ts`:**
- `getAbsolutePosition()` — Recursively resolves nested coordinates by walking the parentId chain
- `findIntersectingContainer()` — Detects valid parent container for reparenting (respects hierarchy rules)
- `syncNodeSubnet()` — Reparents nodes on drag based on spatial intersection

## TypeScript Types (from `src/types/flow.ts`)

```typescript
type AppNode = Node | AwsServiceNodeType | NetworkContainerNodeType

type NetworkContainerType = "region" | "vpc" | "az" | "subnet"
type SubnetType = "Public" | "Private"
type AwsCategory = "Compute" | "Storage" | "Database" | "Networking" | "Security" | "Analytics" | "ML/AI" | "Developer Tools" | "Management" | "Messaging"

type AwsServiceNodeData = {
  name: string
  slug: string
  category: AwsCategory
  serviceId?: string
  description?: string
  fields?: Record<string, string | boolean | number>
}

type NetworkContainerNodeData = {
  containerType: NetworkContainerType
  label: string
  subnetType?: SubnetType
  numberOfVPCs?: number        # Region only
  numberOfAZs?: number         # VPC only
  numberOfSubnets?: number     # AZ only
}
```

## AWS Domain Model

### Service Categories
10 AWS service categories with color-coded styling via `src/config/aws-category-styles.ts`:
- Compute, Storage, Database, Networking, Security, Analytics, ML/AI, Developer Tools, Management, Messaging

### Service Icons
CDN: `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/glincker/thesvg@main/public/icons/{slug}/default.svg`
Fallback: Abbreviation badge (first 2 letters of service name)

### Network Topology
Hierarchy defined in `AGENTS.md` with validation rules:
```
Region → VPC → AZ → Subnet → Service nodes
```

### Container Sizing
Named constants in `src/lib/graph-utils.ts`:
- Region: 900w × 600h
- VPC: 600w × 400h
- Subnet: 320w × 220h
- AZ: auto (fills parent)

## Internationalization (i18n)

All user-visible text is in `src/i18n.ts` with full EN/ES coverage.

**Key naming convention:**
- camelCase keys grouped by feature
- Examples: `sidebarSearch`, `canvasDropHint`, `inspectorServiceConfig`

## Before Commit Checklist

- Run `npm run lint` before committing
- Check Tailwind class usage for canonical forms
- Verify i18n coverage for new UI text
- Follow worktree isolation rules from `AGENTS.md`
